analysis.name: Iterable
analysis.tagline: AI Customer Engagement Platform for Real-Time, Personalized Experiences
analysis.value_proposition: An AI-powered cross-channel customer engagement platform that unifies behavioral data and automates personalized messaging across email, SMS, push, in-app, and more at enterprise scale.
analysis.ideal_customer: Large consumer-facing brands (retail, fintech, marketplaces, consumer apps) with high-volume cross-channel messaging needs and dedicated CRM/lifecycle marketing teams

features.name: email campaigns
features.description: Blast and triggered email campaign sending with link tracking and dynamic sender support
features.evidence_quote: Sending Your First Email Campaign, Email Link Shortening and Click Tracking, Using Dynamic Sender Emails in a Campaign

features.name: sms campaigns
features.description: SMS campaign sending with compliance tools, opt-out/resubscribe management, and customizable instructions
features.evidence_quote: SMS Overview, Best Practices for SMS Compliance, Setting up SMS, Customizing SMS Opt-Out Instructions

features.name: push notifications
features.description: Mobile push notification campaigns for iOS and Android via FCM HTTP v1 API
features.evidence_quote: Creating Push Notification Templates, Sending Push Notifications, Push Campaign Metrics

features.name: in-app messages
features.description: Mobile in-app message campaigns with mobile inbox support
features.evidence_quote: In-App Messages Overview, In-App Messages and Mobile inbox, Creating In-App Templates, Sending In-App Messages

features.name: in-browser messaging
features.description: Web-based in-browser message delivery via Iterable's Web SDK
features.evidence_quote: In-Browser Messaging Overview, Adding Your Website to Iterable, Setting up Iterable's Web SDK, Sending In-Browser Messages

features.name: embedded messaging
features.description: Embeds persistent messages into defined placements within web or app surfaces with eligibility lists
features.evidence_quote: Embedded Messaging Overview, Defining Placements for Embedded Messages, Defining Eligibility Lists for Embedded Messages

features.name: web push notifications
features.description: Browser-based web push notification campaigns
features.evidence_quote: Web Push Overview, Setting up Web Push Notifications, Creating Web Push Templates

features.name: whatsapp campaigns
features.description: WhatsApp message campaigns with template approval management and quality rating tracking
features.evidence_quote: WhatsApp Overview, Setting Up WhatsApp, Managing WhatsApp Template Approvals, WhatsApp Quality Ratings and Messaging Limits

features.name: journey builder
features.description: Visual drag-and-drop multi-step customer journey orchestrator (Studio) with entry/exit logic and branching tiles
features.evidence_quote: Journeys Overview, Journey Components, Studio Basics, Creating a New Journey, Defining How Users Enter Journeys

features.name: ai journey generation
features.description: AI-assisted journey creation that auto-generates journey flows from prompts or goals

features.name: journey delay tiles
features.description: Time-based delay steps within journeys to pace message delivery

features.name: journey filter tiles
features.description: Conditional branching filters within journeys based on user attributes or behavior

features.name: journey user update tiles
features.description: Update user profile fields or list membership as a journey step

features.name: journey integration tiles
features.description: Trigger third-party integrations or webhooks as a step within a journey

features.name: blast campaigns
features.description: One-time broadcast campaigns sent to a static or segmented audience
features.evidence_quote: Campaigns Overview, Choosing an Audience for a Blast Campaign

features.name: template editor (wysiwyg)
features.description: Drag-and-drop visual email and message template builder

features.name: template editor (side-by-side)
features.description: Split-screen code and preview template editor for HTML/text composition

features.name: segmentation
features.description: Audience segmentation based on user profile attributes, events, and behavioral data

features.name: lists and subscriptions
features.description: Static and dynamic list management with subscription preference center support
features.evidence_quote: Lists Overview, Adding Users and Creating Lists, Subscription Preference Centers Overview

features.name: subscription preference centers
features.description: User-facing subscription preference management pages for opt-in/opt-out control

features.name: user profiles
features.description: Unified user profile storage with custom fields and profile field management
features.evidence_quote: Managing User Profiles, Managing User Profile Fields in Iterable, User Profile Fields Used by Iterable

features.name: unknown user activation
features.description: Tracks and engages anonymous/unknown users with retention period and segmentation support
features.evidence_quote: Unknown User Activation Overview, Segmenting Unknown Users, Setting an Unknown User Retention Period

features.name: custom events
features.description: Custom behavioral event tracking with configurable properties and retention settings
features.evidence_quote: Events Overview, Managing Custom Events, Custom Event Properties, Managing Custom Event Retention

features.name: commerce events
features.description: E-commerce specific event tracking including shopping cart items with standard property schema
features.evidence_quote: Managing Commerce Events and Shopping Cart Items, Iterable Commerce Event Properties

features.name: catalog
features.description: Product/content catalog with typed fields and collections for powering dynamic personalized content
features.evidence_quote: Catalog Overview, Catalog Items, Defining Data Types for Catalog Item Fields, Collections, Using Catalogs & Collections in Messages

features.name: data feeds
features.description: External web service data feeds that can be pulled into templates and snippets at send time
features.evidence_quote: Data Feeds Overview, Creating a Data Feed Web Service, Using Data Feeds in Templates, Using Data Feeds in Snippets

features.name: snippets
features.description: Reusable modular content blocks that can be embedded across multiple templates with variable customization
features.evidence_quote: Snippets Overview, Creating and Managing Snippets, Customizing Snippets with Variables, Adding a Snippet to a Template

features.name: handlebars templating
features.description: Handlebars-based merge tag and conditional logic engine for message personalization
features.evidence_quote: Handlebars Overview, Personalizing Templates with Handlebars, Handlebars Reference: Built-In Merge Tags

features.name: dynamic content builder
features.description: Visual tool for generating Handlebars merge tags for dynamic personalized content blocks
features.evidence_quote: Generating Merge Tags with Dynamic Content Builder

features.name: journey live data
features.description: Real-time data injection into journey message steps at moment of send

features.name: sso
features.description: SAML-based single sign-on with support for Azure, Google Workspace, and Okta
features.evidence_quote: Single Sign-On (SSO) Overview, SSO Setup for Common Providers (Azure, Google Workspace, Okta)

features.name: mfa
features.description: Multi-factor authentication enrollment and management for workspace members
features.evidence_quote: Managing and Resetting Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for Iterable Members, Enrolling in Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)

features.name: custom roles
features.description: Role-based access control with custom role creation and permission assignment
features.evidence_quote: Creating and Managing Custom Roles, Permissions for Using Iterable

features.name: projects
features.description: Multi-project workspace organization with per-project settings and unique identifiers
features.evidence_quote: Projects Overview, Project Types and Unique Identifiers, Project Settings

features.name: european data center
features.description: EU data residency option via a dedicated European Data Center for GDPR compliance

features.name: deep links
features.description: Configurable deep link support for email and SMS to route users into native app experiences
features.evidence_quote: Configuring Deep Links for Email or SMS

features.name: https link tracking domains
features.description: Custom HTTPS domains for click tracking links in outbound messages
features.evidence_quote: Configuring HTTPS for Link Tracking Domains

features.name: locales
features.description: Multi-locale/multi-language project configuration for international messaging

features.name: csv list import
features.description: Bulk user list import via CSV file upload

features.name: nova ai intelligence layer
features.description: Umbrella AI platform layer (Nova) providing insights, decisioning, and agent automation across the platform
features.evidence_quote: Nova is Iterable's intelligence layer – helping teams understand what's happening, adapt in real time

features.name: nova agents
features.description: AI agents that automate audience, journey, and campaign building tasks to reduce manual work
features.evidence_quote: Nova agents help teams build and optimize audiences, journeys, and campaigns faster, automating manual work

features.name: nova decisioning
features.description: Real-time AI decisioning engine that evaluates behavioral signals to determine the next best action
features.evidence_quote: Nova continuously evaluates behavioral signals to determine the next best action while keeping teams in control

features.name: nova insights
features.description: AI-powered performance analytics that surfaces signals behind results and highlights where to focus
features.evidence_quote: Nova surfaces the signals behind performance, helping teams understand what's driving results

features.name: predictive goals
features.description: AI feature predicting goal attainment to optimize campaign targeting and timing
features.evidence_quote: Predictive Goals has been an absolute game-changer for our team, maximizing our efficiencies and accelerating time to value

features.name: cross-channel orchestration
features.description: Unified campaign management across email, SMS, push, in-app, web, and WhatsApp from a single platform
features.evidence_quote: create experiences that drive growth across every channel

features.name: real-time ai decisioning
features.description: Processes behavioral and contextual signals in real time to adapt message content and timing
features.evidence_quote: Real-time AI decisioning powered by customer behavior, context, and performance signals

features.name: behavioral data unification
features.description: Unifies behavioral, product, and engagement data into a single customer context layer
features.evidence_quote: Unify behavioral, product, and engagement data so every experience reflects what a customer actually cares about

features.name: data schema management
features.description: Manage and enforce data schema including field types and naming best practices
features.evidence_quote: Data Schema Management Overview, Field Data Types, Best Practices for Field Names and Event Names

features.name: usage and billing dashboard
features.description: In-platform usage and billing metrics tracking for account administrators

features.name: email validation
features.description: Built-in email address validation for user profiles

features.name: user id validation
features.description: User ID format validation and enforcement on ingestion

features.name: user deletion
features.description: GDPR-compliant user profile deletion from the platform

features.name: campaign modernization
features.description: Migration tooling or framework for upgrading legacy campaigns to current campaign architecture

features.name: journey monitoring
features.description: Tools for monitoring journey engagement and performance in real time

features.name: template organization
features.description: Folder or tagging system for organizing templates within the platform

features.name: sms unsubscribes and resubscribes
features.description: Automated handling of SMS opt-out and opt-back-in keywords per carrier compliance

features.name: in-app message recall
features.description: Ability to recall and cancel an in-app campaign after it has been sent

differentiators: AI Customer Engagement Platform with native intelligence layer (Nova) spanning insights, decisioning, and agentic automation
Real-time AI decisioning that adapts to behavioral signals without losing marketer control over goals and guardrails
Enterprise-grade infrastructure trusted for trillions of customer interactions globally
Reduces engineering dependency by giving marketers direct control over audiences, journeys, and experimentation
European Data Center (EDC) for regional data residency

praised: sophisticated segmentation features and comprehensive journey capabilities (Casey Malone, Director CRM @ The Athletic)
Predictive Goals is an 'absolute game-changer' for maximizing efficiencies and accelerating time to value (Lisa Tulloch, Redfin)
Iterable Catalog enabled 7% increase in e-commerce clicks and 80% reduction in email templates (Stanley Black & Decker)
ability to integrate various data feeds in marketing communications and scale campaigns through automation

pricing.notes: Pricing page returned 404; no public pricing available. Platform is demo/sales-led with no self-serve or publicly listed tiers.

pricing.title: Loops - Pricing
pricing.text:
Loops - Pricing
Pricing
Our pricing is based on subscribed contacts synced to Loops. Move the slider to estimate pricing. If you need a custom plan, please
send us an email
.
How many subscribers do you have?
100
1.5m+
1,000
Subscribers
Free
Per month
Early Stage Plan
The free plan is for early products and smaller audiences.
Get started
Up to 4,000 sends/month (enough for weekly emails)
All features included
Includes a small "Powered by Loops" footer
Need a custom solution? Contact us
→
Framer
Replicate
unDraw
perplexity
Modal
clerk
wabi
Spline
Linear
Reuters
Frequently Asked Questions
Wait, what's your price again?
Our pricing is based on subscribed contacts. Transactional email sending is included at no additional charge.
How is a subscriber defined?
A subscribed contact is an email address marked as subscribed. You can view all subscribed and unsubscribed contacts on your Audience page.
What does the free plan include?
The free plan includes up to 4,000 sends a month to 1,000 stored subscribed contacts. We do not charge for additional stored contacts. It also includes Loops branding at the bottom of your emails. Any paid plan will remove these limitations.
Do you charge for team seats?
No.
Do you charge for email sends?
No.
How are you different from other email platforms?
Sending email for your business is important and will have a material impact long term. You should take a look at all your options and go with what's best for your business.
Loops is free to start, with no credit card required and you can invite your entire team to help you make a decision. We believe we've built the best tool in our class. If you need to send email for your SaaS, we can confidently say Loops is likely the right choice.

analysis.name: Loops
analysis.value_proposition: The simplest way to send product, marketing, and transactional email for SaaS teams, from waitlist to IPO.
analysis.ideal_customer: SaaS founders and growth teams who want a single, developer-friendly email platform without per-seat or per-send charges

features.name: email editor
features.description: Visual email editor that renders correctly across all supported email clients
features.evidence_quote: Designs crafted with Loops will work in all supported email clients. Easy.

features.name: email themes
features.description: Customizable themes controlling background, padding, border radius, and border color
features.evidence_quote: Themes — background #1C1917, padding 24px, border 1px solid, radius 6px

features.name: marketing campaigns
features.description: One-time broadcast email campaigns to audience segments

features.name: transactional email
features.description: API-triggered transactional emails sent programmatically, included at no extra cost
features.evidence_quote: Transactional email sending is included at no additional charge.

features.name: lifecycle workflows
features.description: Automated multi-step email sequences with triggers, timers, branches, and filters
features.evidence_quote: Build powerful lifecycle workflows — Trigger userCreated, Timer 5 mins, Send welcome email, Branch

features.name: workflow builder
features.description: Visual loop/flow builder for constructing automation sequences with zoom support
features.evidence_quote: Introducing Workflows — May 7, 2026; Zoom in the loop builder — Apr 1, 2026

features.name: contacts api
features.description: REST API for creating and managing contacts with properties and subscription status

features.name: events api
features.description: API for tracking user events that can trigger automated email workflows

features.name: contact properties
features.description: Custom properties attached to contacts for segmentation and personalization
features.evidence_quote: We have contacts, contact properties, events and event properties.

features.name: event properties
features.description: Custom properties on events enabling fine-grained segmentation and filtering

features.name: segments and filters
features.description: Dynamic audience segments built from contact properties and email engagement
features.evidence_quote: Build segments from properties and engagement, reuse them across campaigns and workflows

features.name: saved segments
features.description: Persistent, reusable segment definitions that stay in sync with product data
features.evidence_quote: Saved segments — Paying Customers 1,240 / Recent Signups 850 / Didn't open last campaign 125

features.name: mailing lists
features.description: Subscription list management and subscriber preference handling

features.name: subscription preferences
features.description: Subscriber-facing preference center for managing opt-ins per list
features.evidence_quote: Lists, Preferences and Slack Notifications — Jul 2, 2024

features.name: double opt-in
features.description: Confirmed opt-in flow requiring subscribers to verify their email address

features.name: goal conversions tracking
features.description: Track email-driven upgrade, churn, and revenue outcomes per campaign/workflow
features.evidence_quote: Connect Loops to your app and track what emails drive upgrades, churn reduction, and revenue.

features.name: a/b split testing
features.description: Experiment framework for testing email variants against conversion goals

features.name: reusable components
features.description: Library of reusable email blocks shared across templates and campaigns

features.name: multi-column layouts
features.description: Side-by-side column layout support in the email editor

features.name: code blocks in editor
features.description: Formatted code block rendering inside email body content
features.evidence_quote: Code blocks in the editor — Apr 3, 2026

features.name: markdown support
features.description: Markdown syntax support for composing email content

features.name: llm translation
features.description: AI-powered automatic translation of email content into other languages

features.name: guardian
features.description: Deliverability and compliance protection system (exact scope unpublished)

features.name: webhooks
features.description: Outbound webhooks triggered by email and contact events
features.evidence_quote: Webhooks, Personal Blocklist and CC/BCC — Feb 5, 2025

features.name: cc/bcc support
features.description: CC and BCC recipient fields on outbound emails
features.evidence_quote: Webhooks, Personal Blocklist and CC/BCC — Feb 5, 2025

features.name: personal blocklist
features.description: Per-account suppression list for blocking specific email addresses
features.evidence_quote: Webhooks, Personal Blocklist and CC/BCC — Feb 5, 2025

features.name: sending domain setup
features.description: Custom domain authentication for improved deliverability

features.name: dns zone file generation
features.description: Auto-generates DNS zone file records for domain authentication

features.name: deliverability tools
features.description: Guides and built-in tooling for improving inbox placement rates
features.evidence_quote: Improve your inbox placement — Read our guide for improving your email deliverability

features.name: smtp
features.description: SMTP relay endpoint as an alternative to API sending

features.name: sdks
features.description: Official client SDKs for Node/JS, TypeScript, Ruby, PHP, Go, and Nuxt
features.evidence_quote: Go, JavaScript, Nuxt, PHP, Ruby — Add Loops to your application in minutes with our SDKs.

features.name: cli
features.description: Command-line interface for managing campaigns, contacts, and components
features.evidence_quote: CLI, Skills and Array support — Apr 16, 2026

features.name: agent skills
features.description: AI agent skill integrations for automated email operations

features.name: unlimited team seats
features.description: No per-seat charges; unlimited team members can be invited at no cost
features.evidence_quote: Do you charge for team seats? No.

features.name: account switcher
features.description: Switch between multiple Loops accounts from a single login

features.name: slack notifications
features.description: Slack alerts triggered by email or contact events
features.evidence_quote: Lists, Preferences and Slack Notifications — Jul 2, 2024

features.name: language support
features.description: Multi-language UI or content support for international sending

features.name: soc2 certification
features.description: SOC2 compliance certification available for security review

features.name: data processing agreement
features.description: DPA and Data Privacy Framework documentation for GDPR/compliance

features.name: email templates
features.description: Pre-built email templates gallery for common use cases

features.name: playbooks
features.description: Pre-configured workflow recipes for common lifecycle automation patterns

features.name: powered-by branding removal
features.description: Remove 'Powered by Loops' footer badge on paid plans
features.evidence_quote: Includes a small 'Powered by Loops' footer... Any paid plan will remove these limitations.

differentiators: No per-seat pricing — unlimited team members at all tiers
No per-send pricing — contact-count-based billing only
Transactional email included at no additional charge
All features available on free plan (no feature gating)
Single platform for product, marketing, and transactional email
SOC2 certified
CLI available for developer automation
Agent/AI skill integrations built-in

tiers.name: Free (Early Stage Plan)

tiers.name: Paid (contact-volume slider)

pricing.notes: Pricing is contact-count-based via an interactive slider — specific dollar amounts were not rendered in scraped content. No seat fees. No email send fees. Transactional email included at all tiers. Custom plans available on request.

analysis.name: ConvertKit
analysis.tagline: Your email list should be working harder for you
analysis.value_proposition: Email marketing automation platform built for creators to grow their audience, automate their marketing, and earn revenue through digital products, paid newsletters, and sponsorships.
analysis.ideal_customer: Individual creators — authors, newsletter writers, coaches, course creators, podcasters, and YouTubers — who want to monetize an email audience

features.name: landing pages
features.description: Create unlimited hosted landing pages to capture new subscribers

features.name: forms
features.description: Embed or host unlimited opt-in forms to grow subscriber list

features.name: email broadcasts
features.description: Send unlimited one-time email newsletters to all or segments of subscribers

features.name: audience tagging
features.description: Apply tags to subscribers to organize and filter the list

features.name: audience segmentation
features.description: Segment subscribers into targeted groups for personalized sends

features.name: digital products
features.description: Sell digital products with automated fulfillment and low transaction fees

features.name: paid subscriptions
features.description: Sell recurring subscription access to newsletters or content

features.name: visual automations
features.description: Drag-and-drop visual workflow builder; 1 basic on free, unlimited on paid

features.name: unlimited visual automations
features.description: Remove the 1-automation cap and build unlimited automated workflows

features.name: email sequences
features.description: Automated drip email series triggered by subscriber actions or time delays

features.name: a/b testing subject lines
features.description: Split-test email subject lines to optimize open rates

features.name: a/b testing content
features.description: Split-test email body content in addition to subject lines (Pro only)

features.name: subscriber polls
features.description: Embed surveys and polls inside emails to gather subscriber feedback

features.name: white label branding
features.description: Remove Kit branding from emails and landing pages

features.name: app store integrations
features.description: Connect third-party creator tools via the Kit App Store

features.name: rss campaigns
features.description: Automatically send emails when new blog or podcast content is published via RSS feed

features.name: email support
features.description: 24/7 email support available on paid tiers

features.name: chat support
features.description: 24/7 live chat support available on paid tiers

features.name: priority support
features.description: 24/7 priority email and chat support, faster response times on Pro

features.name: unlimited team users
features.description: Add unlimited team members and collaborators to the account

features.name: insights dashboard
features.description: Analytics dashboard for tracking subscriber growth and revenue metrics

features.name: deliverability reporting
features.description: Detailed reports on email deliverability, bounce rates, and inbox placement

features.name: subscriber engagement scoring
features.description: Automatically score subscribers by engagement level to identify active vs cold audiences

features.name: collaborative editing
features.description: Multiple team members can co-edit emails simultaneously

features.name: facebook custom audiences
features.description: Sync subscriber segments to Facebook for retargeting ad campaigns

features.name: newsletter referral system
features.description: Built-in referral program to reward subscribers for recommending the newsletter

features.name: edit links in sent broadcasts
features.description: Edit or update hyperlinks in emails after they have already been delivered

features.name: creator network
features.description: Cross-creator recommendation network where creators grow email lists together by recommending each other
features.evidence_quote: Creator Network, where creators grow their email list together

features.name: paid recommendations
features.description: Earn money by recommending other creators' newsletters to your own audience
features.evidence_quote: Earn more on autopilot with your digital products, subscriptions, and Newsletter Sponsorships.

features.name: paid newsletter
features.description: Charge subscribers a recurring fee to access premium newsletter content
features.evidence_quote: Run a paid newsletter — Earn recurring income with newsletter subscriptions

features.evidence_quote: Send beautiful emails that build relationships, not just revenue.

features.description: Infrastructure and tooling to maximize inbox placement rates
features.evidence_quote: Get your emails where they belong—in your subscribers' inboxes.

features.evidence_quote: Subscribers, forms, landing pages, tags, and importing.

features.evidence_quote: Products, Tips, affiliates, and managing your payouts

complaints: No G2 review data available — page did not load in provided sources
No HN or Reddit feature-level complaints surfaced in provided sources; HN discussions focused on business financials and founder story rather than product

free_tier.limits: up to 1,000 subscribers; 1 basic Visual Automation; no email sequences; Kit branding present; no integrations/apps

tiers.name: Newsletter
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1,000 subs; 1 basic automation; unlimited landing pages, forms, broadcasts; tagging & segmentation; commerce

tiers.name: Creator
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1,000 subs; unlimited automations & sequences; A/B subject lines; polls; no branding; app integrations; RSS; 24/7 support

tiers.name: Pro
tiers.headline_inclusions: 1,000 subs; unlimited users; insights dashboard; deliverability reporting; engagement scoring; referral system; edit sent links

pricing.notes: Pricing is subscriber-count-based, not seat-based; prices scale up with list size beyond 1,000. Prices shown ($33/$66) are annualized monthly rates (yearly billing: Creator $390/yr saves $78, Pro $790/yr saves $158 — implying actual month-to-month rates of ~$39 and ~$79 respectively). No enterprise/contact-sales tier; Pro is the top tier. Annual billing advertised as '2 months free' (~17% discount).

Frequently Asked Questions
What are popular marketing automation software features?
Popular features in HubSpot’s marketing software include the ability to:
10x your productivity with content agent and social agent
Build personalized,
automated marketing campaigns
Manage all of your
social media accounts
in a single place
Get your whole team on the same page with campaign management tools
Track your marketing performance with
built-in analytics and custom reporting
How's AI search changing the marketing landscape?
AI search is fundamentally rewriting how users discover content. Chat-based search engines like Perplexity and ChatGPT now answer simple questions before users click any links, intercepting up to 25% of traditional search traffic. Even in traditional Google search, AI overviews occupy an increasing amount of the first page, resulting in fewer clickthroughs.
This creates a new reality for marketers: fewer website visits overall, but those who do visit have significantly higher purchase intent.
AI search has effectively captured part of your top-of-funnel traffic, but it's delivering more qualified clicks. This change means generic content is losing its impact. Your marketing strategy’s success now depends on meeting these more qualified website visitors with relevant, data-driven personalization they can't ignore.
What specific HubSpot tools can help me optimize for AI search?
HubSpot offers several powerful tools to help you adapt to and succeed in the new AI search landscape:
AEO Grader
: Identify content that’s most vulnerable to being easily replicated by AI, and prioritize optimization and personalization accordingly.
Blog research agent [beta]:
Generate blog ideas based on your top-performing posts, focusing on high-intent queries that signal purchase intent rather than generic content that AI can easily replicate.
AEO strategy [beta]
: Get personalized recommendations to help you succeed in the AI-powered search landscape.
Breeze Assistant
: Make it easy for A

Recent changes
This is a running list of notable product changes happening at Substack. For other announcements and company posts, check out our
blog
.
Product Release
5 years ago
Today, we’re releasing a new and improved Subscribers dashboard that helps you understand and interact with your subscribers in important ways.

Read more here:
https://blog.substack.com/p/new-better-analytics-for-your-subscribers
Product Release
5 years ago
We’ve heard from many writers that they’d like more flexibility to customize the appearance of their Substack. Starting today, you’ll see a few new options for theming your newsletter.

Read more here:
https://blog.substack.com/p/new-publication-themes
Product Release
6 years ago
Today, we’re introducing new ways to discover writers on our homepage, and the beta version of Substack Reader, a place to keep up with all your newsletters.

The homepage revamp includes the introduction of lists that display the top newsletters in various categories, as well as a ‘Featured’ section to highlight writers who might not otherwise appear on those lists.

Check out the blog post for more:
https://blog.substack.com/p/new-reader-homepage
Join our team
Founded in 2017, Substack is based in San Francisco and backed by Andreessen Horowitz, Y Combinator, Fifty Years, and other great investors. We're building a better business model for writing.

spec.body:
# ZergStack

**Current rule, updated from Idan Slack feedback on 2026-05-14:** ZergStack / zstack is the infrastructure and runtime stack, not the umbrella brand for user-facing products.

Public brand name is **ZergStack** when referring to the infrastructure product or runtime layer. Lowercase `zstack` is reserved for URLs, filesystem paths, config, and internal shorthand. Older copy that uses "ZergStack" as the product-family umbrella should be treated as stale until rewritten.

Source of truth for managed apps: `~/zerg/zstack.yaml`. Internal control plane: `~/zerg/zstack/` (a mini Render/Railway that deploys to Fly.io and surfaces live status). The public framing is close to Render/Vercel style infrastructure: code push -> deploy, runtime, app hosting, service status, and shared primitives.

## Zerg Products

Default temporary umbrella language for user-facing products is **Zerg products**. Use this until Matt/Idan choose a final family term. Do not use "marketplace" as the default umbrella; it implies third-party contributors and payments before that model is decided.

Current user-facing products / product concepts:

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergboard/Zergboard|Zergboard]] — project / task management (Trello/Linear-style) · competitive: [[Competitive/pm-software/index|pm-software]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergCRM/ZergCRM|ZergCRM]] — internal-only, on roadmap, no early-access flow
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergChat/ZergChat|ZergChat]] — Slack-style team messaging · competitive: [[Competitive/internal-chat/index|internal-chat]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergCal/ZergCal|ZergCal]] — calendar service (humans, interop, agent-native) · competitive: [[Competitive/calendar/index|calendar]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergMeeting/ZergMeeting|ZergMeeting]] — meeting capture / notes / actioning · competitive: [[Competitive/video-meetings/index|video-meetings]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergMail/ZergMail|ZergMail]] — agent-friendly email server · competitive: [[Competitive/workspace-email/index|workspace-email]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergwallet/Zergwallet|Zergwallet]] — wallet for zergs to hold/spend USD/BTC/ETH · competitive: [[Competitive/finance-and-wallets/index|finance-and-wallets]]
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergaudience/zergaudience-audience-taxonomy|Zergaudience]] — inbound capture / contacts / segments layer

Integration narrative: ZergMeeting → Zergboard → ZergChat → ZergCal → ZergMail. Full mapping (data flow, technical foundations, shipped-vs-spec'd status) lives in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Integration|Integration Narrative]].

Cross-product competitive sweeps should now say "Zerg products" unless the claim is specifically about the ZergStack infra layer.

## Apps Managed by ZergStack

These may be deployed via `zstack.yaml`, but that does not automatically make them part of a public product-family brand:

- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZergBox/ZergBox|ZergBox]] — file/upload utility app
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/ZMsg/ZMsg|ZMsg]] — provider-aware messaging gateway (SMS/MMS/WhatsApp via Telnyx, Twilio)

The engineering-platform layer (`Zerg`, `ZDE`, `ZTC`, `Zerg Cloud`, `zterm`) is separate scope from both public Zerg products and the ZergStack infra page. Current internal evidence and open reliability/onboarding findings live in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZTC|ZTC]]; zterm product/backlog direction lives in [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zterm/Zterm|Zterm]].

## Brand & Naming Conventions

- Public/long names: `Zergboard`, `ZergCRM`, `ZergChat`, `ZergCal`, `ZergMeeting`, `ZergMail`, `Zergwallet`, `Zergaudience`, `ZergStack`
- Short names reserved for internal/dev: `zboard`, `zchat`, `zcal`, `zmail`, `zcrm`
- One domain per product (registration tracked under WEB-30 on the Website Zergboard board)
- Product pages can say "runs on ZergStack" when discussing infra/runtime. They should not use "part of ZergStack" as the primary product positioning.
- Green-heavy product pages are not the default visual direction for Zergboard/Zergwallet. Per Idan 2026-05-14, use the more subdued Zergaudience-style dark treatment when using the green palette.

## Pricing

**Locked 2026-05-05** (vampire-attack on individual SaaS competitors):

| Tier | Price | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| Free | $0 | Volume-capped per product |
| Basic | **$1/seat/mo** | Full feature surface; no agent governance |
| Pro | **$9/seat/mo** | + Scoped per-agent API tokens, tenant-safe routes, SSO, audit, advanced roles |
| Enterprise | Custom | + SLA, BYO-KMS, on-prem, SOC 2 / DPA |

Bundle SKUs remain proposed and should be renamed away from "Zstack" in public copy unless Matt/Idan explicitly choose that terminology. Working internal examples: **$4/seat Zerg products Basic** (20% off 5x Basic) and **$19/seat Zerg products Pro** (58% off 5x Pro). Strategy memory: `project_zstack_pricing.md`.

Per-product feature splits: [[Writing/Zstack Microproduct Pricing (WEB-31)]]. Category-leader benchmarks: [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/Pricing-Snapshot|Pricing Snapshot]] (now updated with locked decisions). Implementation rolled out for Zergboard + zmail in PR #283.

## Auth & Security

- Auth unification deferred until product-family bundle launch (WEB-10); provider pre-work under WEB-38
- Security page lives at zergai.com/security; MNDA-gated request flow (no public PDFs)
- SOC 2 Type II target placeholder Q3 2026 (WEB-37)
- DPA / sub-processor list / MNDA template under WEB-34, WEB-35, WEB-36

## Bootstrap Recipe (New Microproduct)

From [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zergwallet/Zergwallet|Zergwallet]]. The recipe generalizes to any new Zerg product:

1. `cd ~/zerg && mkdir <appname> && cd <appname>`
2. `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions`
3. `/rename <appname>`
4. Paste a bootstrap prompt referencing `~/zerg/zergboard` and `~/zerg/zchat` as patterns to mimic (both use the ZTC embedded chat agent)
5. Use a branch like `zstack/<appname>` to keep work isolated

## Related

- [[Epoch/Projects/Zergboard Snapshot|Zergboard Snapshot]] — board-level pull from the zergboard-skill
- [[Epoch/Projects/Product Glossary|Product Glossary]] — context on Zerg, ZDE, ZTC, ZStack as engineering layer
- [[Projects/Zerg-Production/Zstack/ZTC|ZTC]] — internal engineering-platform source of truth and Slack-sourced evidence capture

positioning.elevator_pitch: Zergaudience is an agent-native email marketing layer for founders and small teams who want code-first campaigns, embedded forms, and lifecycle automation without the outages, security incidents, or bloat of legacy platforms.
core_differentiator_lines: Email marketing as code, versioned like your app
Agent-native: your AI can trigger, edit, and audit sends
Runs on ZergStack — same runtime as your product
Mobile app marketing built in, not bolted on
One stack: forms, sequences, broadcasts, no Zapier tax

headline_candidates.headline: Email marketing as code.
headline_candidates.subhead: Campaigns, forms, and lifecycle flows you can diff, review, and deploy.

headline_candidates.headline: The email layer for mobile-first products.
headline_candidates.subhead: Push, in-app, and email lifecycle in one workflow builder — no SDK zoo.

headline_candidates.headline: Send email your agents can actually reason about.
headline_candidates.subhead: Every template, sequence, and trigger is a first-class object, not a WYSIWYG blob.

headline_candidates.headline: Fewer outages. Fewer incident reports.
headline_candidates.subhead: Built on ZergStack — the same runtime your product deploys to.

headline_candidates.headline: One stack from signup form to lifecycle send.
headline_candidates.subhead: Embedded forms, welcome flows, and API-triggered broadcasts without stitching three vendors.

messaging_pillars.promise: Your agents can author, review, and trigger email like a teammate — not through a scraper.
proof_points: Every campaign, template, and sequence exposed as a typed object over API
Diffable YAML/JSON templates instead of opaque WYSIWYG state
Triggers and audiences addressable from ZergChat, Zergboard, and ZergMail flows

messaging_pillars.promise: Ship email the way you ship product — PR, review, deploy, roll back.
proof_points: Templates and workflows live in your repo, not a vendor DB
Answers the 'Show HN: open source Customer.io alternative' demand pattern
Every send has a git SHA and a triggering agent/user attached

messaging_pillars.promise: The whitespace no incumbent owns: real mobile app marketing alongside email.
proof_points: Push, in-app, and email nodes in the same visual workflow builder
One audience layer via Zergaudience — no re-syncing to a mobile SRE tool
Lifecycle events from your app SDK trigger email or push interchangeably

messaging_pillars.pillar: Runs on the same runtime as your product
messaging_pillars.promise: No separate uptime dashboard to babysit; email rides ZergStack.
proof_points: Deployed via zstack.yaml alongside your app services
Directly answers Resend's 12-hour outage and security incident history
Directly answers the 'Customer.io Extended Outage' HN thread
Status surfaces in the same control plane you already watch

messaging_pillars.promise: Campaigns, templates, embedded forms, sequences, and automation — not a stitched MVP.
proof_points: Signup + embedded forms, newsletter publishing, and welcome/lifecycle flows in one product
Visual workflow builder for non-engineers; same graph editable as code
API-triggered broadcasts as a first-class primitive, not an add-on

anti_pitch: We are NOT a WYSIWYG-only newsletter tool like Substack — its editor has been shown to break on inputs as basic as /etc/hosts and its UI has cost users money; agent-native means structured, not painted.
We are NOT a bloated marketing suite like HubSpot Marketing Hub — the historical data-leak thread and marketing-leadership scandals show why enterprise sprawl erodes trust for small teams.
We are NOT another SMTP-first API like Resend that treats reliability and security posture as a post-hoc incident report; we lead with runtime, not with a send button.
We are NOT Customer.io's opaque, closed-config workflow builder — the 'email marketing as code' HN demand exists because that model is frustrating engineers.
We are NOT a creator monetization network like Substack — we don't own your audience, take a rev share, or fight with distribution channels (e.g. Twitter's retweet block on Substack links).
We are NOT ConvertKit-style creator email — the category has almost no product-level feature discussion in public forums; we compete on infrastructure, not on tiers.
positioning.icp_refinement: Founders and 2–20 person product teams — especially those shipping a mobile or agent-integrated app — who already deploy on modern infra (Render/Vercel/Fly style), want lifecycle + newsletter + forms in one place, and view opaque WYSIWYG marketing tools and vendor outages as unacceptable operational risk. Secondary ICP: AI-agent builders who need email as a programmable primitive their agents can safely operate.

objection_handling.objection: Resend / Customer.io / Mailchimp is cheaper or free at our size.
objection_handling.response: Price parity at low volumes is table stakes; the real cost is the second outage or the third vendor you stitch in for forms, mobile, and workflows. Zergaudience collapses that stack onto one runtime you already trust.

objection_handling.objection: HubSpot / Customer.io is more mature and has every feature.
objection_handling.response: Maturity in this category has meant data-leak threads, marketing-leadership scandals, and workflow builders your agents can't touch. We ship the table-stakes surface — campaigns, templates, forms, sequences, automation — with a code-first substrate legacy suites can't retrofit.

objection_handling.objection: Substack / ConvertKit is easier for a non-technical writer.
objection_handling.response: The same visual workflow and newsletter editor is there, but it's a view over structured objects — so when the writer grows into automation or a mobile app, they don't migrate off. And we don't sit between them and Twitter.

objection_handling.objection: Why trust Zerg's uptime over Resend or Customer.io?
objection_handling.response: Because email runs on ZergStack — the same runtime your product already deploys to. There is no separate SaaS status page to babysit; if your app is up, your sends are up, and the control plane shows both.

objection_handling.objection: We already have Customer.io + a mobile push vendor + a forms tool. Why consolidate?
objection_handling.response: Every seam between those tools is a place lifecycle logic breaks and audiences drift. Zergaudience gives you push, in-app, email, forms, and audience in one graph — the mobile app marketing whitespace no incumbent currently owns.

objection_handling.objection: Isn't 'email as code' just a niche for engineers?
objection_handling.response: The 'Show HN: open source Customer.io alternative' pattern shows the demand is real, and the visual builder still exists on top — engineers get diffs and reviews, marketers get the canvas, agents get an API. Nobody is forced into the other's tool.

differentiation_opportunities.name: ZergMeeting → Sequence Autopilot
differentiation_opportunities.what: After a ZergMeeting call ends, an agent reads the transcript, identifies the prospect in ZergCRM, detects deal stage, and auto-drafts a personalized post-meeting email sequence — one approval click to activate
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Sales teams waste 30–60 min per call writing follow-ups; no ESP has a meeting layer, so this gap will never close for them organically
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires ZergMeeting transcripts + ZergCRM deal context + email marketing in a single agent execution context — competitors would need to rebuild 3 separate products and unify their data model, a 12–18 month architectural rewrite
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Every competitor (Mailchimp, Klaviyo, ActiveCampaign) offers 'meeting integrations' only via Zapier → import; no shared agent context means no real personalization from call content

differentiation_opportunities.name: Zergboard Task-Gated Sequence Steps
differentiation_opportunities.what: Email sequence steps can be conditioned on Zergboard card state — e.g., 'send pricing email only when Zergboard card Proposal Ready moves to Done' — enforced natively without webhooks
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Ops teams constantly fight the gap between CRM stages and email triggers; current solutions require Zapier + custom webhook plumbing that breaks on every sprint
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires a native shared event bus between task management and email execution — every competitor's 'integration' is a unidirectional webhook with no state awareness; replicating this requires them to build a PM layer from scratch
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Customer.io's extended HN outage thread surfaced frustration with complex webhook chains; the 'email as code' OSS alternative explicitly cited workflow fragility as the reason to rebuild

differentiation_opportunities.name: ZergChat Campaign War Room
differentiation_opportunities.what: Every email campaign gets a ZergChat channel where the agent posts real-time engagement telemetry; team members can @zerg mid-campaign to issue natural-language commands ('pause sends to the no-open segment', 'write a new subject for the 18–35 cohort') and the agent executes immediately
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Campaign management today is a dashboard-staring exercise with a 24–48 hr loop to act on data; the agent-in-chat surface collapses that to seconds
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires ZergChat's native bot protocol, shared auth between chat and email, and an agent that holds live campaign state — Slack-based competitors (Klaviyo, ActiveCampaign) can approximate this via Slack apps but cannot execute changes without an external API round-trip that breaks on schema changes
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: No scanned competitor has a conversational campaign control surface; all offer 'Slack notifications' (read-only) with no write-back

differentiation_opportunities.name: Cross-Product Engagement Score
differentiation_opportunities.what: Audience scoring that ingests signals from ALL Zstack products: email opens, ZergChat reply patterns, ZergCal meeting attendance, Zergboard ticket activity — producing a single 'engagement heat' score per contact that updates in real time
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Klaviyo and Iterable score on web/email signals only; as B2B buying goes increasingly async across channels, single-channel scoring creates blind spots competitors can't fix without acquiring 3+ product lines
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Signal richness comes from owning the full Zstack data plane — Klaviyo has email + SMS + web; replicating ZergCal attendance or ZergChat engagement would require partnerships or acquisitions that take years to normalize into a unified scoring model
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Iterable markets 'real-time behavioral signals' but all examples are web/mobile events — no calendar or task-layer signals exist in any scanned competitor

differentiation_opportunities.name: Transactional + Marketing Unified Agent Context
differentiation_opportunities.what: Because ZergMail is the actual email server (not a third-party ESP relay), the same agent handles transactional emails (invoices, onboarding, alerts) and marketing sequences — and can suppress, escalate, or personalize marketing based on what the transactional stream reveals (e.g., mute promo sends when a support ticket is open)
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: The market is fragmenting into Resend-for-transactional and Klaviyo-for-marketing; teams pay double and operate two systems; Resend's 12-hr outage + security incident revealed the cost of fragmentation
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires being the MX record owner, not an API relay — Resend, Loops, and Customer.io are all relay layers with no visibility into the inbox state; replicating this requires becoming an ESP, a multi-year infrastructure investment
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Resend HN outage thread + security incident; Customer.io extended outage — both show fragility of relay architecture; HN 'open source Customer.io alternative' post signals developer appetite for owning the full stack

differentiation_opportunities.name: ZMsg Channel-Escalation Protocol
differentiation_opportunities.what: When an email sequence detects cold (N sends, zero opens), the agent automatically escalates to ZMsg for an SMS or WhatsApp touchpoint with a one-liner re-engagement message; if ZMsg gets a reply, the contact re-enters the email sequence at the next step with the SMS reply content injected as personalization context
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Multi-channel orchestration is a top-requested feature across email tools but requires Zapier or a paid add-on; Zerg already owns ZMsg — this is a configuration change, not a new product
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires a native agent that holds cross-channel sequence state (email position + SMS reply content) in a single context — ActiveCampaign and Brevo have SMS but each channel runs its own automation graph with no shared agent memory bridging them
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Brevo markets 'email + SMS + WhatsApp all-in-one' but each channel is a separate automation builder; no competitor has an agent that reasons across channel history to decide the next touchpoint

differentiation_opportunities.name: CRM-Hypothesis A/B Testing
differentiation_opportunities.what: Before writing A/B variants, the agent queries ZergCRM to form a testable hypothesis ('job title = VP Engineering correlates with low CTR on benefit-led subjects — test technical-led variants against this segment only') then auto-concludes and writes back the winning variant + reason to the CRM contact record
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: Every competitor offers AI subject-line writing; none form hypotheses from CRM attributes before writing — the difference is 'AI-assisted copywriting' vs 'AI-driven experiment design'
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires read/write access to a native CRM at hypothesis formation time and result write-back — Mailchimp and Klaviyo have audience data but no native CRM for B2B attribute queries; ActiveCampaign has CRM but no unified agent context linking CRM attributes → test hypothesis → result annotation
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: Klaviyo's 'AI-generated subject lines' feature is widely cloned; no competitor has differentiated on experiment design intelligence vs copywriting assistance

differentiation_opportunities.name: ZergCal Send-Window Intelligence
differentiation_opportunities.what: Send-time optimization informed by ZergCal data: skip sends when a contact has a meeting block (pulled from their shared/public calendar if available), pause sequences when sender team is OOO, and surface optimal send windows based on real recipient timezone calendar density — not statistical averages
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: All competitors offer 'send-time optimization' based on historical open-rate averages; real calendar data is a step-change in signal quality that averages can't approach
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires ZergCal as the calendar provider to have live availability data — Mailchimp and Klaviyo can access Google/Outlook calendar data only via OAuth grants users rarely configure; Zerg is the calendar, so this is always-on with zero setup
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: 'Send time optimization' is a checkbox feature on every scanned competitor's pricing page — identical language, zero differentiation, all using the same historical-average approach

differentiation_opportunities.name: One-Command ESP Migration Agent
differentiation_opportunities.what: A CLI/UI agent that ingests a Mailchimp or Klaviyo export archive, maps lists → ZergCRM segments, reconstructs automations as Zstack agent flows, previews a full diff, and completes migration in under 10 minutes
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: HN 'Show HN: Email marketing as code (OSS Customer.io alternative)' had significant traction — developer frustration with lock-in is at a peak; migration friction is the #1 reason teams stay on inferior tools
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: No competitor builds a tool to migrate users away from themselves; Zerg has no switching-cost reason to avoid building this; once a team migrates their list + automations, re-migration cost is very high
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: OSS Customer.io alternative HN post; Customer.io/Resend outage threads where users expressed desire to leave but cited migration cost as the blocker

differentiation_opportunities.name: Agent-Annotated Suppression with CRM Write-Back
differentiation_opportunities.what: When the email agent suppresses a contact (bounce, unsubscribe, engagement decay), it writes a structured note back to their ZergCRM record with the suppression reason and a recommended re-engagement action — not just a tag, but a reasoning trace future agents can use
differentiation_opportunities.why_now: List hygiene is universally handled as a one-way event log — suppressed contacts become a black hole in every competitor; turning suppression events into CRM intelligence is unrealized across the category
differentiation_opportunities.defensibility: Requires bidirectional agent write access between the email execution layer and CRM — competitors either have no native CRM (Mailchimp, Resend, Loops) or their CRM and email automation are separate execution environments that share data via sync jobs, not live agent context
differentiation_opportunities.evidence: No scanned competitor's suppression documentation mentions CRM write-back or agent reasoning traces; all treat suppression as a deliverability/compliance feature, not a CRM intelligence feature
